NextToken.description: In a paginated request, the token that you can pass in a subsequent request to get the next response page.

ApplicationName.description: The name of the application associated with this environment.

VersionLabel.description: The application version deployed in this environment.

SolutionStackName.description: The name of the SolutionStack deployed with this environment.

TemplateName.description: The name of the configuration template used to originally launch this environment.

EndpointURL.description: For load-balanced, autoscaling environments, the URL to the LoadBalancer. For single-instance environments, the IP address of the instance.

CNAME.description: The URL to the CNAME for this environment.

DateUpdated.description: The last modified date for this environment.

Status.description:
The current operational status of the environment:

  • Launching: Environment is in the process of initial deployment.

  • Updating: Environment is in the process of updating its configuration settings or application version.

  • Ready: Environment is available to have an action performed on it, such as update or terminate.

  • Terminating: Environment is in the shut-down process.

  • Terminated: Environment is not running.

AbortableOperationInProgress.description:
Indicates if there is an in-progress environment configuration update or application version deployment that you can cancel.

true: There is an update in progress.

false: There are no updates currently in progress.

Health.description:
Describes the health status of the environment. AWS Elastic Beanstalk indicates the failure levels for a running environment:

  • Red: Indicates the environment is not responsive. Occurs when three or more consecutive failures occur for an environment.

  • Yellow: Indicates that something is wrong. Occurs when two consecutive failures occur for an environment.

  • Green: Indicates the environment is healthy and fully functional.

  • Grey: Default health for a new environment. The environment is not fully launched and health checks have not started or health checks are suspended during an UpdateEnvironment or RestartEnvironment request.

Default: Grey

HealthStatus.description: Returns the health status of the application running in your environment. For more information, see Health Colors and Statuses.

Resources.description: The description of the AWS resources used by this environment.

Listeners.description: A list of Listeners used by the LoadBalancer.

Tier.description: Describes the current tier of this environment.

Name.description:
The name of this environment tier.

Valid values:

  • For Web server tier – WebServer

  • For Worker tier – Worker

Type.description:
The type of this environment tier.

Valid values:

  • For Web server tier – Standard

  • For Worker tier – SQS/HTTP

Version.description:
The version of this environment tier. When you don't set a value to it, Elastic Beanstalk uses the latest compatible worker tier version.

This member is deprecated. Any specific version that you set may become out of date. We recommend leaving it unspecified.

EnvironmentLinks.description: A list of links to other environments in the same group.

EnvironmentArn.description: The environment's Amazon Resource Name (ARN), which can be used in other API requests that require an ARN.

OperationsRole.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the environment's operations role. For more information, see Operations roles in the AWS Elastic Beanstalk Developer Guide.

S3Bucket.description: The name of the Amazon S3 bucket created.

PlatformSummary.description: Detailed information about the version of the custom platform.

PlatformOwner.description: The AWS account ID of the person who created the platform version.

PlatformStatus.description: The status of the platform version. You can create an environment from the platform version once it is ready.

OperatingSystemName.description: The operating system used by the platform version.

OperatingSystemVersion.description: The version of the operating system used by the platform version.

SupportedTierList.description: The tiers in which the platform version runs.

SupportedAddonList.description: The additions associated with the platform version.

PlatformLifecycleState.description:
The state of the platform version in its lifecycle.

Possible values: recommended | empty

If an empty value is returned, the platform version is supported but isn't the recommended one for its branch.

PlatformVersion.description: The version string of the platform version.

PlatformBranchName.description: The platform branch to which the platform version belongs.

PlatformBranchLifecycleState.description:
The state of the platform version's branch in its lifecycle.

Possible values: beta | supported | deprecated | retired

ResourceQuotas.description: The Elastic Beanstalk resource quotas associated with the calling AWS account.

ApplicationQuota.description: The quota for applications in the AWS account.

Maximum.description: The maximum number of instances of this Elastic Beanstalk resource type that an AWS account can use.

ApplicationVersionQuota.description: The quota for application versions in the AWS account.

Maximum.description: The maximum number of instances of this Elastic Beanstalk resource type that an AWS account can use.

EnvironmentQuota.description: The quota for environments in the AWS account.

Maximum.description: The maximum number of instances of this Elastic Beanstalk resource type that an AWS account can use.

ConfigurationTemplateQuota.description: The quota for configuration templates in the AWS account.

Maximum.description: The maximum number of instances of this Elastic Beanstalk resource type that an AWS account can use.

CustomPlatformQuota.description: The quota for custom platforms in the AWS account.

Maximum.description: The maximum number of instances of this Elastic Beanstalk resource type that an AWS account can use.

ApplicationVersions.description: List of ApplicationVersionDescription objects sorted in order of creation.

NextToken.description: In a paginated request, the token that you can pass in a subsequent request to get the next response page.

ApplicationVersionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the application version.

ApplicationName.description: The name of the application to which the application version belongs.

VersionLabel.description: A unique identifier for the application version.

SourceBuildInformation.description: If the version's source code was retrieved from AWS CodeCommit, the location of the source code for the application version.

SourceLocation.description:
The location of the source code, as a formatted string, depending on the value of SourceRepository

  • For CodeCommit, the format is the repository name and commit ID, separated by a forward slash. For example, my-git-repo/265cfa0cf6af46153527f55d6503ec030551f57a.

  • For S3, the format is the S3 bucket name and object key, separated by a forward slash. For example, my-s3-bucket/Folders/my-source-file.

BuildArn.description: Reference to the artifact from the AWS CodeBuild build.

SourceBundle.description: The storage location of the application version's source bundle in Amazon S3.

S3Bucket.description: The Amazon S3 bucket where the data is located.

S3Key.description: The Amazon S3 key where the data is located.

DateCreated.description: The creation date of the application version.

DateUpdated.description: The last modified date of the application version.

Status.description:
The processing status of the application version. Reflects the state of the application version during its creation. Many of the values are only applicable if you specified True for the Process parameter of the CreateApplicationVersion action. The following list describes the possible values.

  • Unprocessed – Application version wasn't pre-processed or validated. Elastic Beanstalk will validate configuration files during deployment of the application version to an environment.

  • Processing – Elastic Beanstalk is currently processing the application version.

  • Building – Application version is currently undergoing an AWS CodeBuild build.

  • Processed – Elastic Beanstalk was successfully pre-processed and validated.

  • Failed – Either the AWS CodeBuild build failed or configuration files didn't pass validation. This application version isn't usable.

ApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the application.

DateCreated.description: The date when the application was created.

DateUpdated.description: The date when the application was last modified.

Versions.description: The names of the versions for this application.

ConfigurationTemplates.description: The names of the configuration templates associated with this application.

ServiceRole.description:
The ARN of an IAM service role that Elastic Beanstalk has permission to assume.

The ServiceRole property is required the first time that you provide a VersionLifecycleConfig for the application in one of the supporting calls (CreateApplication or UpdateApplicationResourceLifecycle). After you provide it once, in either one of the calls, Elastic Beanstalk persists the Service Role with the application, and you don't need to specify it again in subsequent UpdateApplicationResourceLifecycle calls. You can, however, specify it in subsequent calls to change the Service Role to another value.

MaxCountRule.description: Specify a max count rule to restrict the number of application versions that are retained for an application.

Enabled.description: Specify true to apply the rule, or false to disable it.

MaxCount.description: Specify the maximum number of application versions to retain.

DeleteSourceFromS3.description: Set to true to delete a version's source bundle from Amazon S3 when Elastic Beanstalk deletes the application version.

MaxAgeRule.description: Specify a max age rule to restrict the length of time that application versions are retained for an application.

Enabled.description: Specify true to apply the rule, or false to disable it.

MaxAgeInDays.description: Specify the number of days to retain an application versions.

DeleteSourceFromS3.description: Set to true to delete a version's source bundle from Amazon S3 when Elastic Beanstalk deletes the application version.

SolutionStackName.description: The name of the solution stack these configuration options belong to.

Namespace.description: A unique namespace identifying the option's associated AWS resource.

DefaultValue.description: The default value for this configuration option.

ChangeSeverity.description:
An indication of which action is required if the value for this configuration option changes:

  • NoInterruption : There is no interruption to the environment or application availability.

  • RestartEnvironment : The environment is entirely restarted, all AWS resources are deleted and recreated, and the environment is unavailable during the process.

  • RestartApplicationServer : The environment is available the entire time. However, a short application outage occurs when the application servers on the running Amazon EC2 instances are restarted.

UserDefined.description:
An indication of whether the user defined this configuration option:

  • true : This configuration option was defined by the user. It is a valid choice for specifying if this as an Option to Remove when updating configuration settings.

  • false : This configuration was not defined by the user.

Constraint: You can remove only UserDefined options from a configuration.

Valid Values: true | false

ValueType.description:
An indication of which type of values this option has and whether it is allowable to select one or more than one of the possible values:

  • Scalar : Values for this option are a single selection from the possible values, or an unformatted string, or numeric value governed by the MIN/MAX/Regex constraints.

  • List : Values for this option are multiple selections from the possible values.

  • Boolean : Values for this option are either true or false .

  • Json : Values for this option are a JSON representation of a ConfigDocument.

ValueOptions.description: If specified, values for the configuration option are selected from this list.

MinValue.description: If specified, the configuration option must be a numeric value greater than this value.

MaxValue.description: If specified, the configuration option must be a numeric value less than this value.

MaxLength.description: If specified, the configuration option must be a string value no longer than this value.

Regex.description: If specified, the configuration option must be a string value that satisfies this regular expression.

Pattern.description: The regular expression pattern that a string configuration option value with this restriction must match.

Label.description: A unique name representing this regular expression.

HealthStatus.description: The health status of the environment. For example, Ok.

Causes.description: Descriptions of the data that contributed to the environment's current health status.

InstancesHealth.description: Summary health information for the instances in the environment.

RefreshedAt.description: The date and time that the health information was retrieved.

ManagedActionHistoryItems.description: A list of completed and failed managed actions.

NextToken.description: A pagination token that you pass to DescribeEnvironmentManagedActionHistory to get the next page of results.

ActionId.description: A unique identifier for the managed action.

FailureType.description: If the action failed, the type of failure.

FailureDescription.description: If the action failed, a description of the failure.

ExecutedTime.description: The date and time that the action started executing.

FinishedTime.description: The date and time that the action finished executing.

ManagedActions.description: A list of upcoming and in-progress managed actions.

ActionId.description: A unique identifier for the managed action.

Status.description: The status of the managed action. If the action is Scheduled, you can apply it immediately with ApplyEnvironmentManagedAction.

WindowStartTime.description: The start time of the maintenance window in which the managed action will execute.

Instances.description: The Amazon EC2 instances used by this environment.

LaunchConfigurations.description: The Auto Scaling launch configurations in use by this environment.

LaunchTemplates.description: The Amazon EC2 launch templates in use by this environment.

LoadBalancers.description: The LoadBalancers in use by this environment.

Triggers.description: The AutoScaling triggers in use by this environment.

NextToken.description: In a paginated request, the token that you can pass in a subsequent request to get the next response page.

VersionLabel.description: The application version deployed in this environment.

SolutionStackName.description: The name of the SolutionStack deployed with this environment.

TemplateName.description: The name of the configuration template used to originally launch this environment.

EndpointURL.description: For load-balanced, autoscaling environments, the URL to the LoadBalancer. For single-instance environments, the IP address of the instance.

  • Ready: Environment is available to have an action performed on it, such as update or terminate.

  • Terminating: Environment is in the shut-down process.

  • Terminated: Environment is not running.

true: There is an update in progress.

false: There are no updates currently in progress.

  • Red: Indicates the environment is not responsive. Occurs when three or more consecutive failures occur for an environment.

  • Yellow: Indicates that something is wrong. Occurs when two consecutive failures occur for an environment.

  • Green: Indicates the environment is healthy and fully functional.

  • Grey: Default health for a new environment. The environment is not fully launched and health checks have not started or health checks are suspended during an UpdateEnvironment or RestartEnvironment request.

Default: Grey

Resources.description: The description of the AWS resources used by this environment.

Listeners.description: A list of Listeners used by the LoadBalancer.

Tier.description: Describes the current tier of this environment.

Name.description:
The name of this environment tier.

Valid values:

  • For Web server tier – WebServer

  • For Worker tier – Worker

Type.description:
The type of this environment tier.

Valid values:

  • For Web server tier – Standard

  • For Worker tier – SQS/HTTP

Version.description:
The version of this environment tier. When you don't set a value to it, Elastic Beanstalk uses the latest compatible worker tier version.

This member is deprecated. Any specific version that you set may become out of date. We recommend leaving it unspecified.

EnvironmentLinks.description: A list of links to other environments in the same group.

EnvironmentArn.description: The environment's Amazon Resource Name (ARN), which can be used in other API requests that require an ARN.

OperationsRole.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the environment's operations role. For more information, see Operations roles in the AWS Elastic Beanstalk Developer Guide.

NextToken.description: If returned, this indicates that there are more results to obtain. Use this token in the next DescribeEvents call to get the next batch of events.

VersionLabel.description: The release label for the application version associated with this event.

TemplateName.description: The name of the configuration associated with this event.

EnvironmentName.description: The name of the environment associated with this event.

RequestId.description: The web service request ID for the activity of this event.

InstanceHealthList.description:
Detailed health information about each instance.

The output differs slightly between Linux and Windows environments. There is a difference in the members that are supported under the <CPUUtilization> type.

RefreshedAt.description: The date and time that the health information was retrieved.

NextToken.description: Pagination token for the next page of results, if available.

InstanceId.description: The ID of the Amazon EC2 instance.

HealthStatus.description: Returns the health status of the specified instance. For more information, see Health Colors and Statuses.

Color.description: Represents the color indicator that gives you information about the health of the EC2 instance. For more information, see Health Colors and Statuses.

Causes.description: Represents the causes, which provide more information about the current health status.

LaunchedAt.description: The time at which the EC2 instance was launched.

Duration.description: The amount of time that the metrics cover (usually 10 seconds). For example, you might have 5 requests (request_count) within the most recent time slice of 10 seconds (duration).

RequestCount.description: Average number of requests handled by the web server per second over the last 10 seconds.

StatusCodes.description: Represents the percentage of requests over the last 10 seconds that resulted in each type of status code response.

Status2xx.description: The percentage of requests over the last 10 seconds that resulted in a 2xx (200, 201, etc.) status code.

Status3xx.description: The percentage of requests over the last 10 seconds that resulted in a 3xx (300, 301, etc.) status code.

Status4xx.description: The percentage of requests over the last 10 seconds that resulted in a 4xx (400, 401, etc.) status code.

Status5xx.description: The percentage of requests over the last 10 seconds that resulted in a 5xx (500, 501, etc.) status code.

Latency.description: Represents the average latency for the slowest X percent of requests over the last 10 seconds. Latencies are in seconds with one millisecond resolution.

P999.description: The average latency for the slowest 0.1 percent of requests over the last 10 seconds.

P99.description: The average latency for the slowest 1 percent of requests over the last 10 seconds.

P95.description: The average latency for the slowest 5 percent of requests over the last 10 seconds.

P90.description: The average latency for the slowest 10 percent of requests over the last 10 seconds.

P85.description: The average latency for the slowest 15 percent of requests over the last 10 seconds.

P75.description: The average latency for the slowest 25 percent of requests over the last 10 seconds.

P50.description: The average latency for the slowest 50 percent of requests over the last 10 seconds.

P10.description: The average latency for the slowest 90 percent of requests over the last 10 seconds.

User.description: Percentage of time that the CPU has spent in the User state over the last 10 seconds.

Nice.description:
Available on Linux environments only.

Percentage of time that the CPU has spent in the Nice state over the last 10 seconds.

System.description:
Available on Linux environments only.

Percentage of time that the CPU has spent in the System state over the last 10 seconds.

Idle.description: Percentage of time that the CPU has spent in the Idle state over the last 10 seconds.

IOWait.description:
Available on Linux environments only.

Percentage of time that the CPU has spent in the I/O Wait state over the last 10 seconds.

IRQ.description:
Available on Linux environments only.

Percentage of time that the CPU has spent in the IRQ state over the last 10 seconds.

SoftIRQ.description:
Available on Linux environments only.

Percentage of time that the CPU has spent in the SoftIRQ state over the last 10 seconds.

Privileged.description:
Available on Windows environments only.

Percentage of time that the CPU has spent in the Privileged state over the last 10 seconds.

LoadAverage.description: Load average in the last 1-minute, 5-minute, and 15-minute periods. For more information, see Operating System Metrics.

Deployment.description: Information about the most recent deployment to an instance.

VersionLabel.description: The version label of the application version in the deployment.

DeploymentId.description: The ID of the deployment. This number increases by one each time that you deploy source code or change instance configuration settings.

Status.description:
The status of the deployment:

  • In Progress : The deployment is in progress.

  • Deployed : The deployment succeeded.

  • Failed : The deployment failed.

DeploymentTime.description:
For in-progress deployments, the time that the deployment started.

For completed deployments, the time that the deployment ended.

AvailabilityZone.description: The availability zone in which the instance runs.

PlatformOwner.description: The AWS account ID of the person who created the platform version.

SolutionStackName.description: The name of the solution stack used by the platform version.

DateCreated.description: The date when the platform version was created.

DateUpdated.description: The date when the platform version was last updated.

Maintainer.description: Information about the maintainer of the platform version.

OperatingSystemName.description: The operating system used by the platform version.

OperatingSystemVersion.description: The version of the operating system used by the platform version.

ProgrammingLanguages.description: The programming languages supported by the platform version.

Frameworks.description: The frameworks supported by the platform version.

CustomAmiList.description: The custom AMIs supported by the platform version.

SupportedTierList.description: The tiers supported by the platform version.

SupportedAddonList.description: The additions supported by the platform version.

PlatformLifecycleState.description:
The state of the platform version in its lifecycle.

Possible values: Recommended | null

If a null value is returned, the platform version isn't the recommended one for its branch. Each platform branch has a single recommended platform version, typically the most recent one.

PlatformBranchName.description: The platform branch to which the platform version belongs.

PlatformBranchLifecycleState.description:
The state of the platform version's branch in its lifecycle.

Possible values: Beta | Supported | Deprecated | Retired

SolutionStackDetails.description: A list of available solution stacks and their SolutionStackDescription.

NextToken.description: In a paginated request, if this value isn't null, it's the token that you can pass in a subsequent request to get the next response page.

PlatformName.description: The name of the platform to which this platform branch belongs.

LifecycleState.description:
The support life cycle state of the platform branch.

Possible values: beta | supported | deprecated | retired

BranchOrder.description:
An ordinal number that designates the order in which platform branches have been added to a platform. This can be helpful, for example, if your code calls the ListPlatformBranches action and then displays a list of platform branches.

A larger BranchOrder value designates a newer platform branch within the platform.

SupportedTierList.description:
The environment tiers that platform versions in this branch support.

Possible values: WebServer/Standard | Worker/SQS/HTTP

NextToken.description: In a paginated request, if this value isn't null, it's the token that you can pass in a subsequent request to get the next response page.

PlatformOwner.description: The AWS account ID of the person who created the platform version.

PlatformStatus.description: The status of the platform version. You can create an environment from the platform version once it is ready.

OperatingSystemName.description: The operating system used by the platform version.

OperatingSystemVersion.description: The version of the operating system used by the platform version.

SupportedTierList.description: The tiers in which the platform version runs.

SupportedAddonList.description: The additions associated with the platform version.

PlatformLifecycleState.description:
The state of the platform version in its lifecycle.

Possible values: recommended | empty

If an empty value is returned, the platform version is supported but isn't the recommended one for its branch.

PlatformVersion.description: The version string of the platform version.

PlatformBranchName.description: The platform branch to which the platform version belongs.

PlatformBranchLifecycleState.description:
The state of the platform version's branch in its lifecycle.

Possible values: beta | supported | deprecated | retired

S3Bucket.description: The Amazon S3 bucket where the data is located.

S3Key.description: The Amazon S3 key where the data is located.

DateCreated.description: The creation date of the application version.

DateUpdated.description: The last modified date of the application version.

Status.description:
The processing status of the application version. Reflects the state of the application version during its creation. Many of the values are only applicable if you specified True for the Process parameter of the CreateApplicationVersion action. The following list describes the possible values.

  • Unprocessed – Application version wasn't pre-processed or validated. Elastic Beanstalk will validate configuration files during deployment of the application version to an environment.

  • Processing – Elastic Beanstalk is currently processing the application version.

  • Building – Application version is currently undergoing an AWS CodeBuild build.

  • Processed – Elastic Beanstalk was successfully pre-processed and validated.

  • Failed – Either the AWS CodeBuild build failed or configuration files didn't pass validation. This application version isn't usable.

SolutionStackName.description: The name of the solution stack this configuration set uses.

ApplicationName.description: The name of the application associated with this configuration set.

TemplateName.description: If not null, the name of the configuration template for this configuration set.

EnvironmentName.description: If not null, the name of the environment for this configuration set.

DeploymentStatus.description:
If this configuration set is associated with an environment, the DeploymentStatus parameter indicates the deployment status of this configuration set:

  • null: This configuration is not associated with a running environment.

  • pending: This is a draft configuration that is not deployed to the associated environment but is in the process of deploying.

  • deployed: This is the configuration that is currently deployed to the associated running environment.

  • failed: This is a draft configuration that failed to successfully deploy.

DateCreated.description: The date (in UTC time) when this configuration set was created.

DateUpdated.description: The date (in UTC time) when this configuration set was last modified.

OptionSettings.description: A list of the configuration options and their values in this configuration set.

ResourceName.description: A unique resource name for the option setting. Use it for a time–based scaling configuration option.

Namespace.description: A unique namespace that identifies the option's associated AWS resource.

Value.description: The current value for the configuration option.

ApplicationName.description: The name of the application associated with this environment.

VersionLabel.description: The application version deployed in this environment.

SolutionStackName.description: The name of the SolutionStack deployed with this environment.

TemplateName.description: The name of the configuration template used to originally launch this environment.

EndpointURL.description: For load-balanced, autoscaling environments, the URL to the LoadBalancer. For single-instance environments, the IP address of the instance.

CNAME.description: The URL to the CNAME for this environment.

DateUpdated.description: The last modified date for this environment.

Status.description:
The current operational status of the environment:

  • Launching: Environment is in the process of initial deployment.

  • Updating: Environment is in the process of updating its configuration settings or application version.

  • Ready: Environment is available to have an action performed on it, such as update or terminate.

  • Terminating: Environment is in the shut-down process.

  • Terminated: Environment is not running.

AbortableOperationInProgress.description:
Indicates if there is an in-progress environment configuration update or application version deployment that you can cancel.

true: There is an update in progress.

false: There are no updates currently in progress.

Health.description:
Describes the health status of the environment. AWS Elastic Beanstalk indicates the failure levels for a running environment:

  • Red: Indicates the environment is not responsive. Occurs when three or more consecutive failures occur for an environment.

  • Yellow: Indicates that something is wrong. Occurs when two consecutive failures occur for an environment.

  • Green: Indicates the environment is healthy and fully functional.

  • Grey: Default health for a new environment. The environment is not fully launched and health checks have not started or health checks are suspended during an UpdateEnvironment or RestartEnvironment request.

Default: Grey

HealthStatus.description: Returns the health status of the application running in your environment. For more information, see Health Colors and Statuses.

Resources.description: The description of the AWS resources used by this environment.

Tier.description: Describes the current tier of this environment.

EnvironmentLinks.description: A list of links to other environments in the same group.

EnvironmentArn.description: The environment's Amazon Resource Name (ARN), which can be used in other API requests that require an ARN.

OperationsRole.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the environment's operations role. For more information, see Operations roles in the AWS Elastic Beanstalk Developer Guide.

EnvironmentName.description: The name of the linked environment (the dependency).

PlatformSummary.description: Detailed information about the new version of the custom platform.

Builder.description: The builder used to create the custom platform.

PlatformOwner.description: The AWS account ID of the person who created the platform version.

PlatformStatus.description: The status of the platform version. You can create an environment from the platform version once it is ready.

OperatingSystemName.description: The operating system used by the platform version.

OperatingSystemVersion.description: The version of the operating system used by the platform version.

SupportedTierList.description: The tiers in which the platform version runs.

SupportedAddonList.description: The additions associated with the platform version.

PlatformLifecycleState.description:
The state of the platform version in its lifecycle.

Possible values: recommended | empty

If an empty value is returned, the platform version is supported but isn't the recommended one for its branch.

PlatformVersion.description: The version string of the platform version.

PlatformBranchName.description: The platform branch to which the platform version belongs.

PlatformBranchLifecycleState.description:
The state of the platform version's branch in its lifecycle.

Possible values: beta | supported | deprecated | retired

ApplicationName.description: The name of the application associated with this configuration set.

TemplateName.description: If not null, the name of the configuration template for this configuration set.

DateCreated.description: The date (in UTC time) when this configuration set was created.

DateUpdated.description: The date (in UTC time) when this configuration set was last modified.

OptionSettings.description: A list of the configuration options and their values in this configuration set.

ResourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource for which a tag list was requested.

Ec2InstanceId.description: The Amazon EC2 Instance ID for this information.

SampleTimestamp.description: The time stamp when this information was retrieved.

Message.description:
The retrieved information. Currently contains a presigned Amazon S3 URL. The files are deleted after 15 minutes.

Anyone in possession of this URL can access the files before they are deleted. Make the URL available only to trusted parties.

ApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the application.

DateCreated.description: The date when the application was created.

DateUpdated.description: The date when the application was last modified.

Versions.description: The names of the versions for this application.

ConfigurationTemplates.description: The names of the configuration templates associated with this application.

ServiceRole.description:
The ARN of an IAM service role that Elastic Beanstalk has permission to assume.

The ServiceRole property is required the first time that you provide a VersionLifecycleConfig for the application in one of the supporting calls (CreateApplication or UpdateApplicationResourceLifecycle). After you provide it once, in either one of the calls, Elastic Beanstalk persists the Service Role with the application, and you don't need to specify it again in subsequent UpdateApplicationResourceLifecycle calls. You can, however, specify it in subsequent calls to change the Service Role to another value.

MaxCountRule.description: Specify a max count rule to restrict the number of application versions that are retained for an application.

Enabled.description: Specify true to apply the rule, or false to disable it.

MaxCount.description: Specify the maximum number of application versions to retain.

DeleteSourceFromS3.description: Set to true to delete a version's source bundle from Amazon S3 when Elastic Beanstalk deletes the application version.

MaxAgeRule.description: Specify a max age rule to restrict the length of time that application versions are retained for an application.

Enabled.description: Specify true to apply the rule, or false to disable it.

MaxAgeInDays.description: Specify the number of days to retain an application versions.

DeleteSourceFromS3.description: Set to true to delete a version's source bundle from Amazon S3 when Elastic Beanstalk deletes the application version.

ServiceRole.description:
The ARN of an IAM service role that Elastic Beanstalk has permission to assume.

The ServiceRole property is required the first time that you provide a VersionLifecycleConfig for the application in one of the supporting calls (CreateApplication or UpdateApplicationResourceLifecycle). After you provide it once, in either one of the calls, Elastic Beanstalk persists the Service Role with the application, and you don't need to specify it again in subsequent UpdateApplicationResourceLifecycle calls. You can, however, specify it in subsequent calls to change the Service Role to another value.

MaxCountRule.description: Specify a max count rule to restrict the number of application versions that are retained for an application.

Enabled.description: Specify true to apply the rule, or false to disable it.

MaxCount.description: Specify the maximum number of application versions to retain.

DeleteSourceFromS3.description: Set to true to delete a version's source bundle from Amazon S3 when Elastic Beanstalk deletes the application version.

MaxAgeRule.description: Specify a max age rule to restrict the length of time that application versions are retained for an application.

Enabled.description: Specify true to apply the rule, or false to disable it.

MaxAgeInDays.description: Specify the number of days to retain an application versions.

DeleteSourceFromS3.description: Set to true to delete a version's source bundle from Amazon S3 when Elastic Beanstalk deletes the application version.

ApplicationVersionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the application version.

ApplicationName.description: The name of the application to which the application version belongs.

VersionLabel.description: A unique identifier for the application version.

SourceBuildInformation.description: If the version's source code was retrieved from AWS CodeCommit, the location of the source code for the application version.

SourceLocation.description:
The location of the source code, as a formatted string, depending on the value of SourceRepository

  • For CodeCommit, the format is the repository name and commit ID, separated by a forward slash. For example, my-git-repo/265cfa0cf6af46153527f55d6503ec030551f57a.

  • For S3, the format is the S3 bucket name and object key, separated by a forward slash. For example, my-s3-bucket/Folders/my-source-file.

BuildArn.description: Reference to the artifact from the AWS CodeBuild build.

SourceBundle.description: The storage location of the application version's source bundle in Amazon S3.

S3Bucket.description: The Amazon S3 bucket where the data is located.

S3Key.description: The Amazon S3 key where the data is located.

OptionSettings.description: A list of the configuration options and their values in this configuration set.

ResourceName.description: A unique resource name for the option setting. Use it for a time–based scaling configuration option.

Namespace.description: A unique namespace that identifies the option's associated AWS resource.

Message.description: A message describing the error or warning.

Severity.description:
An indication of the severity of this message:

  • error: This message indicates that this is not a valid setting for an option.

  • warning: This message is providing information you should take into account.

Namespace.description: The namespace to which the option belongs.
